description_long: This is a roughly teardrop-shaped stone with a pointed tip and a broad, rounded base, resembling a primitive hand axe or tool. The surface is uneven and angular, with visible flake scars and chipped edges, suggesting it has been shaped or weathered over time. Its coloration ranges from light tan to medium brown, with some darker patches and subtle mottling across the surface. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or inclusions, and the texture is coarse and gritty to the touch. There are no artificial markings or carvings, but the irregularity of the surface gives it a rugged, natural appearance. This object would likely be found as a loose stone or obstacle in an urban park, garden, or along a landscaped path.
description: A teardrop-shaped, coarse-textured stone with chipped edges and mottled tan-brown coloration, resembling a primitive tool or natural obstacle.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible, showing its teardrop shape and mottled tan-brown surface with chipped edges.
description_view_1: A narrow side profile reveals the stone's thickness and pointed tip, highlighting its angular, flaked edges.
description_view_2: The reverse broad face displays more pronounced surface irregularities and darker mottling, with a rougher texture.
description_view_3: Another side profile, similar to view 1, but with more visible surface pitting and a slightly more angular outline.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and flake scars with no regular or artificial pattern; natural, random surface texture.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped with visible flake scars, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping or other loose stones.
